channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Gunman dead, bystander wounded after shots exchanged near White House, Secret Service says
item.description: The federal law enforcement agency said an individual approached a checkpoint just outside the White House complex and started shooting at posted officers. The Secret Service returned fire, fatally striking the suspect, who died at a hospital. A bystander was also injured in the exchange, according to the Secret Service, which did not detail a motivation for the shooting.

item.title: Gunshots fired near the White House, FBI director says
item.description: “FBI is on the scene and supporting Secret Service responding to shots fired near White House grounds,” FBI Director Kash Patel said on social media. President Donald Trump has been at the White House throughout the day, making calls from the Oval Office.

item.title: 6 money tips new graduates should know, according to our columnist
item.description: There are some basic yet vital things young adults should know to keep their debt low and their net worth growing, Michelle Singletary says.

item.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire deal in final stages, to be ‘announced shortly’
item.description: The president said on social media that the progress was made after calls with several leaders in the Middle East.

item.title: The simple trick that can stop a mosquito bite from itching
item.description: Experts explain how to safely apply heat or cold to curb the urge to scratch an itchy mosquito bite. For milder mosquito bites, over-the-counter options can offer some relief, as will some lotions.

item.title: Claws out as wildlife center, chicken joint owner brawl over planned expansion
item.description: Stan Seymour, who owns 14 Bojangles restaurants in Virginia, and the Southwest Virginia Wildlife Center have been engaged in a protracted legal battle since the center sought to build an aviary for raptors. Combined, the longtime neighbors have spent about $700,000 in their fight.

item.title: Trump’s $1.8 billion payout fund isn’t required to reveal how it actually works
item.description: A five-member panel would distribute the money — compensation for people claiming harm by the justice system — largely shielded from public scrutiny, outside of any evident oversight, and answerable only to the president and his appointees.

item.title: People with eating disorders are taking GLP-1s, and doctors are alarmed
item.description: Providers said they are routinely encountering such patients, some of whom have suffered a relapse and others who have developed an eating disorder for the first time while on the medications.

item.title: Democrats’ rearview mirror is cracked. So is the windshield.
item.description: The autopsy on the 2024 campaign reflects the party's continuing misreading of America.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: The 32 most anticipated movies of the summer
item.description: Spider-Man and the Minions are back, but this season might belong to Odysseus and the aliens.

item.title: Will the Ukraine war force Putin’s exit? David Ignatius explains.
item.description: Adam O’Neal sits down with David Ignatius to discuss how the Ukraine war is going.

item.title: Judge dismisses criminal case against Kilmar Abrego García, calling it 'an abuse of prosecuting power'
item.description: The Maryland man was charged with human smuggling after a high-profile legal fight in which courts ruled he had been illegally deported to El Salvador.

item.title: New rule requires most green-card applicants to apply from outside U.S.
item.description: Immigration lawyers say the change could affect a wide swath of people and marks an escalation in the administration’s efforts to curtail legal migration.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as director of national intelligence
item.description: In a statement posted online, Gabbard said she was leaving the post due to her husband’s diagnosis of a rare bone cancer.

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: My girlfriend refuses to pay me back. Should I break up with her?
item.description: Our advice columnist answers your questions about the strange train we call life.

item.title: Cancer may take my life. Here’s what a hymn made me see.
item.description: A grim prognosis strengthened my belief in providence.

item.title: D.C.’s top baby name picks are revealed, and they lean socially conservative
item.description: The Social Security Administration released data for last year’s most popular names. Baby naming experts weighed in on the city’s picks.

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.
item.description: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.

item.title: Everything that happened on Stephen Colbert’s star-studded final episode of ‘The Late Show’
item.description: The comedian wrapped up the CBS franchise with surprise celebrity guests, a visit from his fellow late-night hosts and multiple songs.

item.title: Economic confidence is plunging, poll finds, a potential midterm problem for Trump
item.description: Americans’ confidence in the economy has hit a nearly four-year low, threatening Republicans ahead of the midterm elections as President Donald Trump struggles to deliver on affordability pledges.

item.title: Republican blowup. AI order. Kamala Harris. Ebola flight. Wet weekend. Colbert’s send-off. Summer movies. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Pressure from Silicon Valley helped block Trump's expected order on AI
item.description: Industry leaders warned in last-minute calls to the president that the proposed safety vetting system could inhibit development of the pivotal technology.

item.title: Trump tried to silence late-night hosts. They’re mocking him even more.
item.description: A Washington Post analysis shows hosts are telling more jokes about Trump, despite threats by the FCC chair and the president’s repeated calls to get them fired.

item.title: Republicans are going from dumb to dumber
item.description: Republicans are going from dumb to dumber

item.title: Senate Republicans, frustrated over Trump’s $1.8B settlement fund, delay a vote on paying for immigration enforcement
item.description: Senate Republicans peppered acting attorney general Todd Blanche with questions about the fund during a lunch meeting Thursday, according to two Senate GOP aides familiar with the meeting.

item.title: U.S. bears brunt of Israel’s missile defense, Pentagon assessments show
item.description: Amid hostilities with Iran, the American military expended far more advanced interceptors to protect Israel than Israeli forces did, according to Defense Department data.

item.title: Trump delays executive order on AI oversight, hours before planned signing
item.description: The White House had already sent out invitations to the event, where the president had been expected to sign an order increasing government scrutiny of new artificial intelligence models.

item.title: A live TV gaffe turned the ‘Survivor 50’ finale into a fitting train wreck
item.description: An inadvertent spoiler from host Jeff Probst ensured that a season filled with missteps would end with the biggest one of all.

item.title: Federal arts commission, packed with Trump allies, approves his towering triumphal arch
item.description: The president rejected the idea of lowering the structure’s height, an architect said. Military veterans have sued to stop the project.

item.title: David Ignatius: Don’t believe Trump and Xi’s narrative. America keeps falling uphill.
item.description: Hold off on those announcements of the dawn of the Chinese century.

item.title: Democrats release contentious autopsy of 2024 presidential campaign
item.description: The Democratic National Committee had planned to keep its report private to avoid infighting.

item.title: An Ivy League school in today’s On the Record
item.description: An Ivy League school in today’s On the Record

item.title: Ebola responders say aid cuts by the U.S. and other Western nations left them ill-equipped to contain virus
item.description: Medical personnel in the Democratic Republic of Congo know what it takes to get an Ebola outbreak under control. But aid groups and health officials say they lacked the staff, surveillance systems and emergency supplies needed to quickly detect early infections or contain the virus as cases surged in recent days.

item.title: Trump’s legal fund. D.C. arch. Ebola concerns. Raúl Castro. SpaceX finances. Layoff mirage. Colbert’s farewell. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Our critic watched hundreds of hours of children’s TV shows — and found the best one
item.description: A journey through the weird and wonderful world of television for kids under 6 yields a clear winner. And it’s not the show you’re probably thinking of.

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX discloses massive losses ahead of IPO
item.description: The rocket company, set to debut on the stock market in coming weeks, has recorded $13 billion in losses since 2023, according to a financial filing posted by the Securities and Exchange Commission. It was the public’s first glimpse at what may soon be Musk’s most valuable company.

item.title: Trump officials say they can build 250-foot arch without Congress’s approval
item.description: To build a triumphal arch near Arlington Cemetery, the administration hopes to rely on a 100-year-old authorization for a different project.

item.title: The unintended consequences of mass deportations
item.description: The unintended consequences of mass deportations

item.title: White House resisted letting doctor with Ebola return to U.S.
item.description: The administration’s reluctance to bring an infected American back to U.S. soil marked a sharp contrast with the 2014 West Africa Ebola outbreak.

item.title: Former Cuban president Raúl Castro is indicted in U.S. on murder and conspiracy charges
item.description: The charges in the deadly downing of two jets in 1996, likely to remain symbolic, come as the Trump administration has ratcheted up pressure on Cuba.

item.title: Longest-serving D.C. schools chancellor to leave for education nonprofit
item.description: Lewis D. Ferebee, who was appointed in 2018 and oversaw the district’s post-pandemic recovery in math and reading scores, will become CEO of EdReports.

item.title: I caught my in-law’s ‘vegan’ partner eating a burger. Hax readers give advice.
item.description: After busting in-law’s preachy partner as pseudo-vegan, letter writer wants to burn special menus and serve him the ashes.

item.title: Why do diners get bad tables, even when it’s not busy? Ask our food critic your dining questions.
item.description: Washington Post food critic Elazar Sontag entertains your restaurant questions, rants and raves.

item.title: Harvard faculty vote to limit A grades for undergraduates
item.description: The result announced Wednesday is the most prominent symbol of a reckoning at some elite schools concerned by the increasing number of A’s — a widespread issue that some faculty members warn is fundamentally damaging the integrity of education.

item.title: James Murdoch, son of media mogul Rupert Murdoch, buys New York magazine and news site Vox
item.description: Several other Vox titles are not included in the transaction and will make up a second, independent company under a new name.

item.title: Trump’s revenge in Kentucky could cost the GOP
item.description: Thomas Massie’s defeat sharpens a dangerous instinct before the midterms.

item.title: Barney Frank, influential congressman and gay rights hero, dies at 86
item.description: The Massachusetts Democrat led a sweeping overhaul of the financial industry after the Great Recession. He was one of the first openly gay congressmen in history.

item.title: Two hosts in today’s On the Record
item.description: Two hosts in today’s On the Record

item.title: Takeaways from primaries where Trump demonstrated power over GOP
item.description: It was a big night for the president as his favorites swept Republican foes in Georgia, Pennsylvania and Kentucky.
